topNews.title: Show HN: A Karpathy-style LLM wiki your agents maintain (Markdown and Git)
topNews.rawTextSnippet: I shipped a wiki layer for AI agents that uses markdown + git as the source of truth, with a bleve (BM25) + SQLite index on top. No vector or graph db yet. It runs locally in ~/.wuphf/wiki/ and you can git clone it out if you want to take your knowledge with you. The shape is the one …
topNews.summary:
• What happened: A new wiki layer for AI agents was launched, utilizing markdown and git for knowledge management.  
• Why it matters: This tool enhances the organization and accessibility of AI-generated information, promoting better collaboration among agents.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this technology to improve their AI systems' knowledge retention and sharing capabilities.

topNews.title: Show HN: LLMs consume 5.4x less mobile energy than ad-supported web search
topNews.rawTextSnippet: The standard AI energy debate compares server-side LLM inference to a server-side Google query. I think this misses most of what actually happens on a mobile device during a real search session. I built a parametric model of the full end-to-end mobile search session: 4G/5G radio energy, SoC ren…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: LLMs use 5.4 times less mobile energy than ad-supported web searches.  
• Why it matters: This finding highlights the efficiency of LLMs in mobile environments, potentially reducing energy consumption.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage LLMs to create more sustainable mobile applications and services, appealing to eco-conscious consumers.

dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, OpenAI has launched GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro, enhancing its API offerings and potentially reshaping enterprise applications with improved capabilities. Meanwhile, a new study highlights that large language models (LLMs) consume 5.4 times less mobile energy than traditional ad-supported web searches, suggesting a more sustainable approach for businesses leveraging AI for customer engagement. In a surprising move, SAP has diverged from the prevailing trend among enterprise platforms by opting against AI agents, raising questions about its long-term strategy in a rapidly evolving market. Additionally, a new project inspired by Karpathy aims to create a wiki for LLMs maintained by agents, showcasing the growing interest in collaborative AI development. These developments underscore the dynamic nature of AI technology and its implications for operational efficiency and strategic decision-making in businesses.
